free_text: Measurements of the Coulomb dissociation cross section
of 156 MeV Li6 projectiles at extremely low relative
fragment energies of astrophysical interest
Quadrupole-quadrupole- dipole-sextupole
(QQDS) magnet configuration with a 60.degr.
deflecting dipole magnet of 1.5 m curvature radius.
monitor detector
All spectra are corrected for the background of random
coincidences.
For the overall absolute cross-section
normalization, an uncertainty of 15% is estimated,
taking into account the uncertainty in the target
thickness and detection efficiency.
SB
SD: Subent 008 added. Correction in all
Subents (COMMON moved to Subents 002-007).
The Pb targets used were self-supporting metallic
foils of 4.0 and 6.7 mg/cm**2 thickness, respectively,
and an isotope enrichment more than 99%.
Double-differential cross section as function
of dOmega -> (alpha,d) from table II
No information about source of uncertainty
Table II from Phys.Rev.,C44(1991)2195
SD: EN from Subent 001 added. BIB updated.
The Pb targets used were self-supporting metallic
foils of 4.0 and 6.7 mg/cm**2 thickness, respectively,
and an isotope enrichment more than 99%.
Table II from Phys.Rev.,C44(1991)2195
SD: EN from Subent 001 added. BIB updated.
The Pb targets used were self-supporting metallic

and an isotope enrichment more than 99%.
Negative and positive relative energies
denote backward and forward emission, respectively, of
the a particle in the Li-6 c.m. system.
